titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 7162

p.seg: Malcolm king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls and barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts and officials, French and English, Gaelic and Galwegian, clerk and lay, and all men of his entire land, greeting.

Know that after I assumed knighthood, I gave, granted, and by this my charter made firm, to Walter son of Alan, my steward, Birkenside and Legerwood by their correct boundaries, that is to say, fully and entirely, just as King David, my grandfather held the said lands in demesne. I have also given Mow to the said Walter by its correct boundaries, and with all its lawful pertinents, for him and his heirs to hold and to have of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as any earl or baron holds and possesses any land of me freely, fully, and honourably, performing for the said lands to me and my heirs the service of one knight.

With these witnesses, Arnold the bishop of St Andrews, Herbert the bishop of Glasgow, John the abbot of Kelso, William the abbot of Melrose, Obser the abbot of Jedburgh, Walter the chancellor, William the king's brother, Richard de Morville, Gilbert de Umfraville, Waldeve the brother of Earl Cospatric, Jordan Ridel.
